steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The word "Currently" is the key clue — it signals that the action is happening in the present time, right now.

steps.title: Match the verb tense to the time signal
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: verb tense. Rule: the time adverb "Currently" demands a simple present tense verb for an ongoing state.
  • Choice A — "will have hung": future perfect tense; describes an action completed at a future point, contradicting "currently." ✗
  • Choice B — "hangs": simple present tense; correctly describes the painting's ongoing location right now. ✓
  • Choice C — "hung": simple past tense; places the action in the past, contradicting "currently." ✗
  • Choice D — "was hanging": past progressive tense; describes a past ongoing action, contradicting "currently." ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
